response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with genuine developer-tooling interest (IDE component work on LabWindows/CVI, test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators), but his core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not Java/Kotlin. He lists Java only as a secondary skill, shows no Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or JVM-internals experience, and his IDE work was C/C++ based and brief. He falls short of the role's hard requirements around deep JVM expertise and the enterprise Java ecosystem.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI components)
Strong developer-tooling track record (test runner, assertion library, MCP generators)
Experience with low-latency, large-scale systems and solid engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or deep JVM expertise; Java appears only peripherally
No Spring or enterprise Java framework experience
Recent work centered on web/full-stack TypeScript, far from enterprise IDE/JVM tooling
